{{Quote|It wasn't until the disastrous raid on Fara's Belt that the Imperial Navy's fear of the B-wing was dispelled.|Wedge Antilles, "Alliance Strategy and Tactics Lecture 137: Combat Starfighters"|<ref name="RAS2" />}}
'''Fara's Belt''' was a [[Realspace/Legends|space]] location in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories']] [[Rolion sector/Legends|Rolion sector]]. During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], it was the site of a [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] [[Unidentified Imperial communications station|communications station]]. In [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]], the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] planned to [[Raid on Fara's Belt|attack]] the facility, although [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Imperial Intelligence]] learned of the upcoming operation—though not which of the Empire's Rolion [[Sector/Legends|sector]] bases specifically would be attacked. Nevertheless, a ruse by Imperial Intelligence's [[Major/Legends|Major]] [[Herrit]] allowed the Empire to learn that the Fara's Belt installation was the target, with an Imperial ambush there subsequently annihilating the attacking Rebel [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[Squadron/Legends|squadron]].

==Description==
Fara's Belt was a location in [[Realspace/Legends|space]]<ref name="RAS2" /> within the [[Rolion sector/Legends|Rolion sector]],<ref name="EAOC" /> a part of the [[Trans-Hydian/Legends|Trans-Hydian]] portion of<ref name="TEA" /> the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="EAOC" />

==History==
{{Quote|For example, when first introduced by Admiral Ackbar, the Rebel B-wing starfighter was far superior to anything the Imperials had at the time. But in the Rebel raid on Fara’s Belt, the small squadron of B-wings was wiped out by massive numbers of inferior TIE fighters.|Wedge Antilles, "Alliance Strategy and Tactics Lecture 137: Combat Starfighters"|<ref name="RAS2" />}}
[[File:Rolion_sector.jpg|thumb|left|250px|The communications station in Fara's Belt was one of the Galactic Empire's 752 bases in the Rolion sector (pictured).]]
During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], Fara's Belt contained a [[Unidentified Imperial communications station|communications station]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].<ref name="RAS2" /> In [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]],<ref name="0 ABY">Per [[The Rebel Alliance Sourcebook (Second Edition)|''The Rebel Alliance Sourcebook'', Second Edition]], the [[raid on Fara's Belt]] took place between the entering of [[A/SF-01 B-wing starfighter/Legends|A/SF-01 B-wing starfighters]] in [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] service and the [[Evacuation of Yavin|end]] of [[Jan Dodonna/Legends|Jan Dodonna's]] service with the Alliance, events that are dated to [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]] by ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' and ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'', respectively.</ref> [[General/Legends|General]] [[Jan Dodonna/Legends|Jan Dodonna]] of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] planned<ref name="RAS2" /> a hit-and-run [[Raid on Fara's Belt|attack]]<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|I|254|Fara's Belt}}</ref> on the facility using the Alliance's new [[A/SF-01 B-wing starfighter/Legends|A/SF-01 B-wing starfighters]]. [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Imperial Intelligence]] learned of the Alliance planning an attack on one of its 752 bases in the Rolion [[Sector/Legends|sector]] but did not know which one of them was the target. [[Major/Legends|Major]] [[Herrit]] of Imperial Intelligence then contacted all of those installations and instructed each of them to report a different common systems malfunction—in the case of the Fara's Belt communications station, "gravitational fluctuation malfunction"—to the central Imperial headquarters.<ref name="RAS2" />

Imperial Intelligence subsequently intercepted a Rebel transmission mentioning the malfunction reported by the Fara's Belt base, which allowed for the Empire to set up an ambush there. When the Rebel B-wing [[Squadron/Legends|squadron]] emerged from [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] above the communications station, it was immediately surrounded and attacked by an overwhelming number of [[TIE series/Legends|TIE fighters]], including both standard [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE/LN starfighters]] and [[TIE/IN interceptor/Legends|TIE/IN interceptors]], which proceeded to annihilate the Rebel force. Later, the Alliance's [[Wing Commander/Legends|Wing Commander]] [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]] mentioned the raid on Fara's Belt in "[[Alliance Strategy and Tactics Lecture 137/Legends|Alliance Strategy and Tactics Lecture 137: Combat Starfighters]]," a [[Hologram/Legends|holo-lecture]] on Rebel [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] strategy and tactics aimed at mid-level Alliance military and [[Politics/Legends|political]] leaders.<ref name="RAS2" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:FarasBelt-TEA.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The erroneous map placement of Fara's Belt in ''The Essential Atlas'']]
Fara's Belt was first mentioned in [[Paul Murphy]]'s [[1990]] ''[[The Rebel Alliance Sourcebook]]'', which was published by [[West End Games]] for use with ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''. In the book, the location was illustrated by [[Allen Nunis]].<ref name="RAS">''[[The Rebel Alliance Sourcebook]]''</ref>

The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed it in grid square Q-6.<ref name="TEA" /> Although the [[StarWars.com]] [[Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion|Online Companion]] to that book later placed Fara's Belt in the Rolion sector<ref name="EAOC" />—information that had originated in ''The Rebel Alliance Sourcebook''<ref name="RAS" /> and had been repeated in other sources<ref name="RAS2" /><ref name="CSWE" />—[https://web.archive.org/web/20180325111127/https://www.starwars.com/news/essential-atlas-outer-rim-sectors The Essential Atlas Extra: Outer Rim Sectors] feature of the Online Companion depicted the territory of that sector<ref name="EAOC Outer Rim">{{SW|url=news/essential-atlas-outer-rim-sectors|text=The Essential Atlas Extra: Outer Rim Sectors|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref> and the location of Fara's Belt—as shown on a map in ''The Essential Atlas''<ref name="TEA" />—as not intersecting. Instead, the feature depicted the location of Fara's Belt as falling within the territory of the [[Nuiri sector/Legends|Nuiri sector]], which this article assumes is incorrect.<ref name="EAOC Outer Rim" />
